name: Merricks Hotel
signature: A low-key country local on the Merricks Beach side  -  walkable from the holiday houses and exactly what a sandy afternoon needs
editorNote:
The Merricks Hotel is a low-key country pub on the Merricks Beach side of the Peninsula  -  far enough off the main road to feel like a local's find, close enough to the beach that you can walk from the holiday houses in the afternoon. The menu is straightforward bistro  -  parma, schnitzel, a fish of the day, a proper steak sandwich  -  and the service is the kind of unhurried country-pub welcome that makes a long sandy afternoon feel complete.

This is not destination dining and nobody is pretending otherwise. It is the pub you walk to when the holiday house feels too quiet and the beach has done its job for the day. On a Friday night in summer, with the deck full of locals, it is exactly what a Peninsula pub should be.

Walk in. Order the fish and chips. Do not overthink it.

heroImage.alt: Country pub imagery evocative of Merricks Hotel, Mornington Peninsula
